The target trial requires patients to have a documented gBRCA1 or gBRCA2 mutation and be on a first-line platinum-based regimen for metastatic pancreas cancer, whereas prior trials focus on ovarian cancer and require completion of first-line or multiple lines of platinum-based therapy, indicating a mismatch in cancer type and treatment stage. Severity: 1.0

The target trial allows patients who have received platinum as potentially curative treatment for a prior cancer, provided at least 12 months have elapsed, while prior trials exclude patients who have received chemotherapy for any abdominal or pelvic tumor, highlighting a difference in prior treatment eligibility. Severity: 0.9

The target trial includes patients with stable disease, whereas prior trials exclude patients with stable disease on the post-treatment scan, showing a discrepancy in disease stability criteria. Severity: 0.8

The target trial requires a minimum of 16 weeks of continuous platinum treatment without progression, while prior trials require patients to be randomized within 8 weeks of their last dose of chemotherapy, indicating a difference in timing and treatment duration requirements. Severity: 0.7

The target trial excludes any previous treatment with a PARP inhibitor, while prior trials also exclude previous treatment with PARP inhibitors, but the focus on different cancer types and stages may lead to different patient populations. Severity: 0.6
